title: daisybio/domainsplit pipeline parameters
description: A nextflow pipeline to split domain-domain interaction data into train, validation, and test while reducing data leakage and bias.

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Unused samplesheet placeholder; this pipeline reads inputs from the url_* parameters instead.
input.help_text: Kept for nf-core template compatibility. Source databases are configured via the url_* parameters in nextflow.config.

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

source_database_options.title: Source database URLs
source_database_options.description: URLs of the public source databases used to assemble the domainsplit database.

url_3did.description: URL of the 3did SQL dump archive.

url_uniprot_id_mapping.description: URL of the UniProt id-mapping file (per organism).

url_uniprot_embeddings.description: URL of the per-residue ProtT5 UniProt embeddings HDF5.

url_uniprot_go_terms.description: URL of the UniProt GO terms TSV (REST stream).

url_uniprot_sequences.description: URL of the UniProt-SwissProt FASTA archive.

url_negatome.description: URL or path of the Negatome non-interaction TSV.

url_string.description: URL of the STRING protein-protein links file.

url_pfam2go.description: URL of the Pfam2GO mapping file.

url_pfam_template.description: Template URL for downloading per-Pfam alignment archives; `{pfam_id}` is substituted per family.

download_prott5_complete.description: ProtT5 embeddings download strategy: true = single full HDF5 download; false = chunked async REST queries for required IDs only.

url_prott5_embeddings_complete.description: URL of the full UniProt-SwissProt per-residue ProtT5 embeddings HDF5 (used when download_prott5_complete=true).

esm_protein_shards.description: Number of FASTA shards for parallel per-residue ESM embedding generation over the protein sequences. Each shard runs as one GPU task; cluster QoS caps concurrency (see slurm.config).

esm_domain_shards.description: Number of FASTA shards for parallel pooled ESM embedding generation over the domain sequences. ~15k domains comfortably fit in 1 shard.

esm_batch_size_protein.description: Initial batch size for protein per-residue ESM inference. Halved in-process on torch.OutOfMemoryError and retried.

esm_batch_size_domain.description: Initial batch size for domain pooled ESM inference. Domains are short, so this can be larger than the protein batch size.

esm_max_len.description: Maximum sequence length fed to ESM. Longer sequences are skipped (quadratic-attention long tail; would otherwise OOM or stall a chunk).

esm_smoke_test.description: If true, each ESM chunk processes only the first 100 records. For quick end-to-end wiring tests on the cluster.

esm_hf_cache_dir.description: Shared HF_HOME directory for the ESM chunk tasks (e.g. an NFS path). Avoids parallel chunks each re-downloading the gated ESM3 / ESMC weights. Set empty to use the per-task local cache.

smoke_test_n_ddis.description: If set, after DDI collection the domainsplit DB is reduced to this many total interactions — floor(N/2) random positive DDIs and floor(N/2) random negative DDIs. All downstream steps then run on a tiny data set. Leave null (default) to disable the smoke filter.

negative_ppi_parquet.description: Optional path to a Y2H/MS PPI parquet (columns: gene_name_bait, gene_name_prey, n_tested, ...). When set, COLLECT_DDI_DATA derives extra negative DDIs by mapping bait/prey genes to UniProt then Pfam (via url_uniprot_id_mapping) and inserting the most-frequent Pfam-pair candidates restricted to domains already in positive DDIs. Null (default) disables this source.

negative_ppi_min_n_tested.description: Minimum value of the `n_tested` column required to keep a PPI row when building negative DDIs from negative_ppi_parquet.

negative_ppi_source_label.description: Value written to the `source` column of domain_domain_interaction for DDIs derived from negative_ppi_parquet.

pfam_download_batch_size.description: Number of Pfam IDs grouped into a single download job to reduce scheduler overhead.

prott5_chunk_size.description: Number of protein IDs per REST API chunk when download_prott5_complete is false.

negative_sampling_strategy.description: Strategy for sampling negative DDIs from the PPI parquet source.

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.

help_full.description: Display the full detailed help message.

show_hidden.description: Display hidden parameters in the help message (only works when --help or --help_full are provided).
